political.s: No specific political PAC or lobbying data found for Dollar Car Rental. As a subsidiary of Hertz Global Holdings, political activities would be consolidated under parent company reporting.

charity.s: Dollar operates under Hertz Corporation's charitable framework which supports education, environment, safety and travel initiatives. Parent company Hertz accepts grant applications from nonprofit organizations with a 90-day funding cycle focused on children and families.

environment.s: Dollar invests in fuel-efficient vehicles and alternative fuel options. Parent company has incorporated electric and hybrid vehicles into fleets and offers carbon offset programs. Environmental sustainability reporting is managed at the parent Hertz/Enterprise level with mixed documented progress.

labor.s: Employee reviews on Glassdoor (3.1/5 stars) and Indeed show mixed satisfaction with concerns about low wages, management quality, and work-life balance. Hertz acquisition in 2012 reportedly worsened conditions. 44% would recommend to a friend; compensation rated 2.8/5 and career opportunities 2.5/5.

dei.s: No specific DEI initiatives or stance information found for Dollar Car Rental. DEI policies would be managed through parent company frameworks.

animals.s: Not applicable - car rental service does not involve animal testing or animal-related products.

guns.s: Not applicable - car rental service does not sell or manufacture firearms.

privacy.s: Dollar's privacy policy was updated 7/1/2023. Company collects personal data for vehicle rental operations and shares information with service providers. California residents have supplemental privacy protections. Policy compliance appears mixed based on customer complaints about unauthorized charges and data handling.

execPay.s: Not disclosed - Dollar Car Rental is a private subsidiary of Hertz Global Holdings. Executive compensation data not publicly available.
